methods.description: Process for analyzing water samples using ICP-MS and ICP-OES to determine the concentration of various elements, ensuring precision and accuracy with certified reference materials and internal standards.

steps.description: Water samples were analyzed by ICP-MS using a 7700 Agilent for the elements: As, Ba, Cd, Cr, Co, Cu, Mg, Mn, Mo, Ni, Pb, Sr, Ti, V, Zn and REE (lanthanides and actinides), and ICP-OES 5110 Agilent for the elements: Fe, Na, K, Ca, Si, P, S.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze water samples using ICP-MS and ICP-OES for element concentration

steps.description: International certified reference materials USGS GXR-1, GXR-2, GXR-4, and GXR-6 were analyzed at the beginning and end of each batch of samples.
steps.searchable_summary: validate analysis with certified reference materials

steps.description: Internal control standards (Rh) were analyzed every ten samples and a duplicate was run for every ten samples.
steps.searchable_summary: use internal control standards for quality control

steps.description: The different solutions were prepared with Milli-Q (18.2 MΩ, 25°C) water.
Sulphates were determined by ion chromatography.
steps.searchable_summary: determine sulphate concentration using ion chromatography

methods.description: Procedure for preparing and analyzing diatom samples to determine species abundance and diversity using microscopy and the Shannon-Wiener index.

steps.description: Live diatom samples were examined in a light microscope to exclude the possibility of having dead diatoms and avoid abundance errors.
steps.searchable_summary: examine live diatom samples under a microscope

steps.description: An aliquot (after cleaning off the formalin) was treated with hydrogen peroxide (H2O2 30%) and heat was applied to accelerate the reaction, followed by three centrifugations (1500 rpm) to wash off the excess of H2O2.
steps.searchable_summary: clean diatom samples with H2O2 and centrifuge

steps.description: Permanent slides were mounted using the high refractive index medium Naphrax®.
Diatoms were identified and semi-quantified under a light microscope using a 100X objective to determine relative abundances of species.
steps.searchable_summary: identify and quantify diatoms under a microscope

steps.description: Abundance and total number of species were used in the diatom assemblage diversity calculation through Shannon-Wiener index.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate diatom diversity using Shannon-Wiener index

methods.description: Statistical technique used to analyze the relationships between variables, including physicochemical parameters, richness, and diversity, to understand the mineral-water-biota interaction process.

steps.description: Two more variables were introduced: richness and diversity, as input variables.
steps.searchable_summary: include richness and diversity as input variables for factor analysis

steps.description: The ∑Ficklin has been integrated into a single variable without disturbing the solvency of the model.
steps.searchable_summary: integrate ∑Ficklin into a single variable for analysis

steps.description: The REE are also presented as a summatory.
steps.searchable_summary: present REE as a summatory for analysis

steps.description: Two factors were defined, and not set in the field, to explain most of the variables' variability.
steps.searchable_summary: define two factors to explain variable variability

steps.description: The two factors rotated by the varimax rotation method.

methods.description: Procedure for collecting water and diatom samples from various sites to characterize them hydro-geochemically and biologically.

steps.description: Water samples were taken to be hydro-geochemically and biologically characterized.
Fifteen representative sampling points were chosen to cover a wide range of pH.
steps.searchable_summary: select sampling points based on pH range

steps.description: Water and diatom samples were collected both in mine-influenced and non-mine-influenced sites.
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples from mine-influenced and non-mine-influenced sites
